mechanism: The Merton-model credit-equity basis (implied IG ~140-180bp vs. actual ~90-110bp) is the quantitative expression of the broader cross-asset pricing disagreement as seen through the equity-credit lens. The widest gap since 2007 implies credit is pricing a fundamentally different world than equity volatility suggests.
regime_dependence: The basis has historically resolved via credit widening (5/7 episodes). Resolution requires a catalyst — typically recession, financial stress, or forced recognition event.
